title: A market vendor I remember
summary: Describe a specific vendor at a market who made an impression on you.
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences describing a vendor you remember from a market. Describe what they sold, what they looked like, and one thing they said or did. Use present simple for habits and past simple for the specific memory.
hintStarters: At the market near my old apartment, there was a man who…
I remember him because he once…
The thing I liked most about her stall was…
sampleText: At the market near my old apartment in Marrakech, there was a man called Hassan who sold dried fruit and nuts from a long wooden stall. He was a small, thin man with a grey beard and very quick hands. His stall was the most colourful one in that part of the market because he arranged everything in big, careful piles and covered them with cloth at night. He always knew when something was seasonal and told his customers which product was fresh that week. Hassan recognised most of his regular customers and remembered what they usually bought. I went there every Friday morning, and after my third visit he remembered that I always wanted the apricots, not the dates. He kept a small pile aside for me. Once, I watched him explain to a tourist why the price was fair instead of just reducing it. He was calm and clear about it, and the tourist left with a big bag and a smile. The day I moved away from the neighbourhood, I stopped at his stall and told him. He did not say much. He wrapped a bag of almonds and handed it to me and said, 'For the road.' I still think about that.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: hoa quả sấy và các loại hạt

sampleAnnotations.phrase: arranged everything in big, careful piles
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: sắp xếp mọi thứ thành những đống lớn, gọn gàng

sampleAnnotations.phrase: covered them with cloth at night
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: phủ vải lên chúng vào ban đêm

sampleAnnotations.phrase: kept a small pile aside for me
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: để dành một đống nhỏ cho tôi
bình tĩnh và rõ ràng về điều đó

sampleAnnotations.phrase: left with a big bag and a smile
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: rời đi với một túi lớn và nụ cười
Để ăn đường (câu nói tặng quà khi ai đó lên đường)
Tôi vẫn còn nghĩ đến điều đó

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Mixing present simple habits and past simple memories
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Trong bài này, present simple dùng để mô tả thói quen thường xuyên trong quá khứ ('He always knew', 'recognised'), trong khi past simple dùng cho các sự kiện cụ thể ('I watched him', 'He wrapped'). Cả hai thì đều đúng ngữ cảnh vì toàn bộ đang hồi tưởng.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: In this piece, present simple describes the vendor's regular habits ('He always knew', 'recognised'), while past simple marks specific events ('I watched him', 'He wrapped'). Both are correct because the whole piece is a memory.

sampleTranslationVi: Tại khu chợ gần căn hộ cũ của tôi ở Marrakech, có một người đàn ông tên Hassan bán hoa quả sấy và các loại hạt từ một sạp gỗ dài. Ông là một người đàn ông nhỏ, gầy với bộ râu xám và đôi tay nhanh nhẹn. Quầy hàng của ông là quầy đẹp nhất ở khu vực đó của chợ vì ông sắp xếp mọi thứ thành những đống lớn, gọn gàng và phủ vải lên chúng vào ban đêm. Ông luôn biết khi nào thứ gì đó theo mùa và nói với khách hàng sản phẩm nào tươi trong tuần đó. Hassan nhận ra hầu hết khách quen và nhớ họ thường mua gì. Tôi đến đó mỗi sáng thứ Sáu, và sau lần thứ ba ông nhớ rằng tôi luôn muốn mơ khô, không phải chà là. Ông để dành một đống nhỏ cho tôi. Một lần, tôi thấy ông giải thích cho một khách du lịch tại sao giá cả là hợp lý thay vì chỉ giảm giá. Ông bình tĩnh và rõ ràng về điều đó, và người khách du lịch rời đi với một túi lớn và nụ cười. Ngày tôi chuyển khỏi khu phố, tôi ghé vào quầy hàng của ông và nói với ông. Ông không nói nhiều. Ông gói một túi hạnh nhân và đưa cho tôi và nói: 'Để ăn đường.' Tôi vẫn còn nghĩ đến điều đó.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct use of 'there was' to introduce a character.
options: There was a man who sold dried fruit.
There were a man who sold dried fruit.
There is a man who sold dried fruit.
There has been a man who sold dried fruit.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'There was' (singular) is correct for introducing one person in the past.
mcQuestions.hint: Is the subject singular or plural? What past form does 'there' take?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly says something a person always did?
options: He always knew when something was seasonal.
He always know when something was seasonal.
He always has known when something was seasonal.
He always was knowing when something was seasonal.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Knew' is past simple. 'Know' is present; 'has known' is present perfect; 'was knowing' is incorrect — 'know' is a state verb and does not use continuous forms.
mcQuestions.hint: Is 'know' an action verb or a state verb? Can state verbs use the continuous?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the word that means food produced only at a particular time of year.
options: colourful
seasonal
regular
worn
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Seasonal' means available or fresh only at a certain time of year. 'Regular' means consistent; 'colourful' means bright in colour; 'worn' means damaged by use.
mcQuestions.hint: Which word is related to the seasons of the year?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence that uses 'instead of' correctly.
options: He explained the price instead just reducing it.
He explained the price instead of just reducing it.
He explained the price instead to just reducing it.
He explained the price instead for just reducing it.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Instead of' is a fixed preposition phrase, followed by a noun or verb+ing. 'Instead of just reducing it' is correct.
mcQuestions.hint: What word follows 'instead' to introduce an alternative?

mcQuestions.prompt: Select the correct sentence about a vendor remembering a customer.
options: He remembered that I always wanted the apricots.
He remembered that I always want the apricots.
He remembered what I always want.
He remembered as I always wanted the apricots.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Remembered that + past clause' is correct. The verb in the reported clause shifts to past: 'wanted', not 'want'.
mcQuestions.hint: After a past reporting verb, what tense goes in the clause?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the phrase that means a vendor saved something specifically for you.
options: He kept a small pile aside for me.
He kept a small pile away from me.
He kept a small pile off for me.
He kept a small pile back to me.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Keep something aside for someone' means to reserve or save it for them. 'Away from' means at a distance.
mcQuestions.hint: Which preposition phrase follows 'keep' to mean 'save or reserve'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the sentence that correctly uses 'wrap' in the past.
options: He wrapped a bag of almonds and handed it to me.
He wraped a bag of almonds and handed it to me.
He wrapping a bag of almonds and handed it to me.
He has wrapped a bag of almonds and handed it to me.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Wrapped' is the correct past simple. 'Wraped' is a misspelling. 'Wrapping' is present participle. 'Has wrapped' is present perfect, inconsistent with the past narrative.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the correct past simple spelling of 'wrap'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best describes someone who buys from the same place regularly?
options: a seasonal vendor
a regular customer
a worn visitor
a daily buyer
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Regular customer' is the standard phrase for someone who returns to the same shop or stall often.
mcQuestions.hint: What do you call someone who buys from the same place many times?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence that correctly uses 'tell' to give information.
options: He told his customers which product was fresh that week.
He told to his customers which product was fresh that week.
He said his customers which product was fresh that week.
He told his customers what product is fresh that week.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Tell someone + what/which clause' is the correct pattern. 'Tell to someone' is incorrect. 'Said someone' is incorrect — 'say' does not take an indirect object.
mcQuestions.hint: Does 'tell' take a direct object (a person) before the clause?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct sentence about someone leaving a place.
options: The tourist left with a big bag and a smile.
The tourist left with a big bag and smiled.
The tourist leaved with a big bag and a smile.
The tourist was leaving with a big bag and a smile.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Left' is the irregular past simple of 'leave'. 'Leaved' is incorrect. 'Was leaving' (continuous) changes the meaning.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the irregular past simple of 'leave'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence where 'recognise' is used correctly.
options: He recognised most of his regular customers.
He recognised the most of his regular customers.
He recognised most his regular customers.
He was recognising most of his regular customers.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Recognised most of his customers' is correct. 'The most of' is non-standard. 'Recognise' is a state verb and does not use continuous forms.
mcQuestions.hint: Is 'recognise' an action or state verb? Does 'most of' need 'the'?

criticalThinkingQuestion: Is there a person at a market, shop, or street corner that you remember clearly but never really spoke to? What made them stay in your memory?
